children.text: Setup decisions become less exploitable under public lethal pressure.
The scorer already considers setup under pressure.
The roadmap tightens narrow cases where visible HP bands and public KO pressure say a boost turn is likely dead unless it immediately changes the route.
A setup move can still be a tempting scorer result in edge cases if the immediate public danger is underpriced.
Planned gate: low-HP setup loses unless setup wins, survives, or preserves a route.
The player can sometimes exploit passive boost turns by attacking through them.
The boss uses the public board to choose damage, denial, or preservation sooner.
No private damage roll or hidden move slot is needed.
Uses visible HP/status, public threat masks, revealed moves, and boss-owned state only.

children.name: source-footer
children.text: This is a later roadmap item, not the first implementation patch.
